rows.who: Wants to add a feature and ship it green without tripping an enforced guard; reads CONTRIBUTING + EXTENDING and runs the green gate.
rows.job: Add a feature as a leaf and ship it green, knowing the enforced rules up front so the guard teaches instead of ambushes.

affordances.need: the build-on-fak guide (the Register* seams + the leaf path)
a scaffolder that stamps a conforming leaf (add a leaf, don't edit core)
a one-command green gate documented at the entry point (the feedback loop)
the enforced rules surfaced up front (trunk-only, out-of-tree write guard) so the guard teaches
structured issue templates so a contribution starts well-formed
a 'good first issue' on-ramp so a first-timer has a starting point

gaps: The CLA is a draft pending Netra's legal review (CONTRIBUTING.md) — the sign-off ritual is staged, not yet binding on an external PR

rows.who: An autonomous coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, an MCP client) that lands in the repo cold and must discover, adopt, and build on fak.
rows.job: Discover what fak is, adopt it, and build on it cold — the deep audit of this is the agent-readiness scorecard.

affordances.need: the inlined full doc-map an answer engine ingests
the deep agent-readiness scorecard (16 KPIs, friction-debt) this row delegates to

gaps: Depth lives in agent-readiness (discover/adopt/build, friction-debt) — this row is the roster entry, not the full 13-KPI audit
